title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
How to Buy Term Insurance without Medical Test in India?
A term insurance plan is sought to get financial security against the uncertain risk of premature death. It insures your life and promises a guaranteed payment in the event of an unforeseen event that claims your life. This payment provides a financial cushion to your family and helps them take care of their needs even when you are not around. Moreover, the low premium of term insurance plans enables you to opt for high coverage levels for maximum protection.
While insurance companies do not restrict the coverage amount of a term plan, they scrutinise high-value policies minutely before issuing them. Insurers insist on medical tests to assess your health in the case of high-value plans and also if you are above a specified age.
Many policyholders want to avoid these medical tests considering them to be a hassle. As such, they look for term insurance plans without medical tests. Are such plans available?
Buying term insurance without medical test
A medical test is necessitated only in one or both of the following instances –
You opt for a high sum assured
You are older, i.e., 35 or 40 plus
However, if you are younger and/or you buy a term insurance plan of a limited sum assured, you would not have to undergo any medical tests. There are term plans available in the market that you can buy without medical tests. The coverage is issued instantly, and you can get the plan conveniently.
How to buy term insurance without a medical test?
You can visit the online website of insurance companies to buy term insurance without medical tests. These plans are mostly available online and can be bought by filling up an online proposal form. You would also have to fill up a short medical questionnaire stating your current health and medical status.
If you have standard health, you abstain from lifestyle habits like drinking and smoking, you are young, and you opt for a low sum assured, the policy would be issued instantly. You would not have to undergo any medical tests.
What factors to consider before buying term insurance without a medical test?
When buying term insurance without a medical test, you should consider the following factors –
The sum assured granted:
In term plans without medical coverage, the sum assured is usually restricted. So, check the sum assured that the insurer is offering without the requirement of medical tests. Assess if the available sum assured is optimal for your financial needs or not.
The coverage term:
You should also check the policy tenure allowed by the plan. Since term plans usually pay only a death benefit during the policy term, opting for the maximum possible tenure is better.
The premium amount:
The premium amount of such policies that do not require medical tests might be lower. This is because the coverage is lower. However, if there are any lifestyle habits or if you suffer from a medical complication, your premium might be increased.
Availability of riders:
Though term insurance plans allow riders for additional coverage, the availability of riders might be restricted when medical tests are not done. So, check for the available riders and choose those that are suitable for your protection needs.
Full disclosure:
Though the insurance company does not require medical tests in some cases, it does not mean that you can lie or hide important information when filling up the proposal form. You should disclose all your details in the proposal form even when you have medical complications or if you smoke or drink. This would help you get the right coverage and avoid any possibility of claim rejections.
Is it worth buying a term plan without a medical test?
While a term plan without a medical test can be convenient and grant instant coverage, there might be coverage restrictions. You might not be able to enjoy an optimal amount of sum assured as the insurance company would limit its risk when the scope of medical underwriting is limited in the absence of medical tests.
So, while you might get the coverage, it might not be sufficient. Also, the insurer might charge a higher premium for covering the risks that cannot be verified through medical tests.
Drawbacks of buying term insurance without a medical exam
Here are some of the drawbacks of buying a term insurance policy without undergoing a medical exam –
Lesser Coverage
The first and major drawback of not undergoing medical tests is limited coverage. The sum assured of such plans is limited. This might not grant you the coverage that you seek for your family’s security. As such, the plan might fall short in terms of its coverage.
Higher Premium
Since the insurance company does not assess your health risk, it charges a high premium to compensate for the higher health risk that it might be underwriting in issuing the policy. As such, you would have to pay a higher premium for the policy.
Claim Rejection
There are increased chances of claim rejection if the insurer does not know your existing medical conditions. If you lie about such conditions or hide them when buying the policy, you breach the principle of utmost good faith. As such, in the case of death due to a hidden medical condition, the insurer might reject your claim due to a breach of good faith.
Does giving a medical test benefit while buying term insurance?
Yes, undergoing a pre-entrance health screening while buying a term insurance plan is better and beneficial. The reasons are as follows –
Easier to get the claim approved
When you undergo a medical test, the insurer knows about any hidden or existing medical complication that you might suffer from. If the insurer knows the existing medical complications and still issues the policy, it would not be able to reject any claim if it occurs due to the discovered medical issues. As such, it becomes easier to get your claims approved.
Lower premiums
If there are no medical complications, the insurer is assured of your health. As such, the insurance company does not charge any additional premium to compensate for the risk of the unknown. This leads to lower premiums if you are healthy.
Get extensive coverage
Since your health risk can be assessed, insurers do not cap the sum assured that you can avail of. As such, you can opt for an extensive scope of coverage and secure optimal protection for yourself and your family.
Benefits of Term Insurance Plans
You can unlock the complete benefits of term insurance plans when your risks are known to the insurer. You can get inbuilt as well as optional riders, longer coverage tenures, high sum assured and complete protection against unforeseen contingencies.
Affordable Premiums
Lower premiums are charged for healthy lives. So, if you are free from illnesses or medical complications or when you are young, you can enjoy optimal coverage at affordable premiums.
Income Tax Benefits
As you get to avail of a higher sum assured with medical tests, the premium is higher. This allows you to claim a higher deduction under Section 80C subject to the limit as specified therein. A higher deductible means a lower tax liability so that you can save more in taxes.
Death Benefit
The death benefit under the plan is also high since you were able to choose a higher sum assured for coverage when you bought the plan.
Survival Benefit
If you choose the return of premium term plan, the survival benefit would also be higher since higher premiums would be refunded back.
Rider Benefits
Term plans with medical tests allow you to add one or more optional riders to the base policy. This addition enhances the scope of coverage, providing complete protection.
Conclusion
While term plans without medical check-ups are available in India, it is better to go for medical check-ups. This would allow you to choose the optimal sum assured that would give complete financial protection to you and your family when you are not around.
